---
title: "Chapter 10 - Vibhuti Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">विभूतियोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-09.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-11.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-10-1" data-reference="10.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-1">10.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Once more, O mighty-armed one, hear my supreme word, which I shall speak to you, who are dear to me, desiring your good.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भूय एव महाबाहो श‍ृणु मे परमं वचः ।<br>
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-2" data-reference="10.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-2">10.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Neither the hosts of gods nor the great sages know my origin; for I am the source of the gods and of the great sages in every way.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः ।<br>
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-3" data-reference="10.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-3">10.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who knows me as unborn and beginningless, the great lord of the worlds, is undeluded among mortals and freed from all sins.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम् ।<br>
असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-4" data-reference="10.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-4">10.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Understanding, knowledge, freedom from delusion, patience, truth, self-restraint, and calm; pleasure and pain, becoming and passing away, fear and fearlessness;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसम्मोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः ।<br>
सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-5" data-reference="10.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-5">10.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">nonviolence, evenness, contentment, austerity, charity, fame and disrepute—these varied states of beings arise from me alone.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः ।<br>
भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-6" data-reference="10.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-6">10.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The seven great sages of old, and the four Manus too, sharing my nature, were born of my mind; from them sprang these creatures of the world.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा ।<br>
मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-7" data-reference="10.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-7">10.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who knows in truth this glory and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of mine is yoked with unshaken <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>; of this there is no doubt.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ।<br>
सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-8" data-reference="10.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-8">10.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the origin of all; from me everything proceeds. Knowing this, the wise, filled with feeling, worship me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते ।<br>
इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-9" data-reference="10.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-9">10.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Their minds set on me, their lives given over to me, enlightening one another and ever speaking of me, they are content and glad.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम् ।<br>
कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-10" data-reference="10.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-10">10.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">To these, ever yoked to me and worshiping me with love, I give that <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of understanding by which they come to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम् ।<br>
ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-11" data-reference="10.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-11">10.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Out of compassion for them, dwelling within their hearts, I destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the shining lamp of knowledge.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः ।<br>
नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-12" data-reference="10.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-12">10.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">You are the supreme <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, the supreme abode, the supreme purifier; the eternal divine person, the primal god, unborn and all-pervading.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान् ।<br>
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-13" data-reference="10.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-13">10.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">So all the sages declare you—the divine seer Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa—and you yourself tell me so.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा ।<br>
असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-14" data-reference="10.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-14">10.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I hold all this that you tell me to be true, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." title="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="keshava">Keshava</span>; for neither the gods nor the danavas know your manifestation, O Bhagavan.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव ।<br>
न हि ते भगवन्व्यक्तिं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-15" data-reference="10.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-15">10.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">You alone know yourself by yourself, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." aria-label="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." title="purusha: Person, spirit, or conscious principle; meaning varies by passage." data-glossary-id="purusha">Purushottama</span>—source of beings, lord of beings, god of gods, lord of the world.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम ।<br>
भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-16" data-reference="10.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-16">10.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Tell me, then, in full, of your divine glories, by which you pervade these worlds and abide in them.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः ।<br>
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-17" data-reference="10.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-17">10.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">How shall I know you, O yogin, by constant meditation? In what forms, O Bhagavan, are you to be contemplated by me?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन् ।<br>
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-18" data-reference="10.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-18">10.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Tell me again, in detail, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." title="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="janardana">Janardana</span>, of your <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> and your glories; for I am not sated with hearing this nectar.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन ।<br>
भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि श‍ृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-19" data-reference="10.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-19">10.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">So be it: I shall tell you of my divine glories—but only the chief of them, O best of the Kurus, for there is no end to my extent.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः ।<br>
प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-20" data-reference="10.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-20">10.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the true Self (<span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." aria-label="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." title="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." data-glossary-id="atman">Atman</span>), O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." aria-label="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." title="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." data-glossary-id="gudakesha">Gudakesha</span>, seated in the heart of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of beings.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः ।<br>
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-21" data-reference="10.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-21">10.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among the Adityas I am Vishnu; among lights, the radiant sun;<br>
among the Maruts I am Marichi; among the stars, the moon.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान् ।<br>
मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-22" data-reference="10.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-22">10.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span> I am the Sama <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Veda</span>; among the gods, Vasava;<br>
among the senses I am the mind; in beings I am consciousness.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः ।<br>
इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-23" data-reference="10.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-23">10.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among the Rudras I am Shankara; among the yakshas and rakshasas, the lord of wealth; among the Vasus I am fire; among mountains, Meru.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम् ।<br>
वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-24" data-reference="10.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-24">10.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among household priests, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, know me to be their chief, Brihaspati; among generals I am Skanda; among waters, the ocean.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम् ।<br>
सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-25" data-reference="10.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-25">10.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among the great sages I am Bhrigu; among utterances, the single syllable <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." aria-label="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." title="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." data-glossary-id="om">Om</span>; among sacrifices, the sacrifice of silent repetition; among the unmoving, the Himalaya.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम् ।<br>
यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-26" data-reference="10.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-26">10.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among all trees I am the ashvattha; among divine sages, Narada;<br>
among the gandharvas, Chitraratha; among the perfected, the sage Kapila.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः ।<br>
गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-27" data-reference="10.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-27">10.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know me, among horses, as Uchchaihshravas, born of the nectar; among lordly elephants, Airavata; and among men, the king.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम् ।<br>
ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-28" data-reference="10.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-28">10.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among weapons I am the thunderbolt; among cows, the wish-granting cow;<br>
among begetters I am Kandarpa; among serpents, Vasuki.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक् ।<br>
प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-29" data-reference="10.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-29">10.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among the nagas I am Ananta; among water-dwellers, Varuna;<br>
among the ancestors, Aryaman; among those who govern, Yama.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम् ।<br>
पितॄणामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-30" data-reference="10.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-30">10.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among the daityas I am Prahlada; among reckoners, time itself; among beasts I am the lion, and among birds, Garuda.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम् ।<br>
मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-31" data-reference="10.31">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-31">10.31</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among purifiers I am the wind; among warriors, Rama; among fishes I am the makara, and among rivers, the Ganges.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम् ।<br>
झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-32" data-reference="10.32">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-32">10.32</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Of creations I am the beginning, the end, and the middle, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>; of branches of knowledge, the knowledge of the self; among debaters, I am the reasoned argument.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन ।<br>
अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-33" data-reference="10.33">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-33">10.33</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Of letters I am the letter A; of compounds, the dual;<br>
I am imperishable time, and the sustainer facing in every direction.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च ।<br>
अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताहं विश्वतोमुखः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-34" data-reference="10.34">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-34">10.34</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am all-devouring death, and the origin of things to come;<br>
among feminine powers I am fame, fortune, speech, memory, wisdom, steadfastness, and patience.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम् ।<br>
कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-35" data-reference="10.35">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-35">10.35</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Of the Sama chants I am the Brihat-sama; of meters, the Gayatri; of months I am Margashirsha; of seasons, the flowering spring.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम् ।<br>
मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकरः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-36" data-reference="10.36">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-36">10.36</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the gambling of the deceitful and the splendor of the splendid; I am victory, I am resolve, I am the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span> of the good.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ।<br>
जयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-37" data-reference="10.37">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-37">10.37</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among the Vrishnis I am <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." aria-label="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." title="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." data-glossary-id="vasudeva">Vasudeva</span>; among the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pandavas</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>;<br>
among sages I am Vyasa; among seers, Ushanas the seer.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनञ्जयः ।<br>
मुनीनामप्यहं व्यासः कवीनामुशना कविः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-38" data-reference="10.38">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-38">10.38</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among those who chastise I am the rod; among those who seek victory, statecraft; among secrets I am silence; and I am the knowledge of the knowing.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम् ।<br>
मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-39" data-reference="10.39">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-39">10.39</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And I am the seed of all beings, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>; there is nothing moving or unmoving that could exist without me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन ।<br>
न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-40" data-reference="10.40">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-40">10.40</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">There is no end to my divine glories, O scorcher of foes; what I have declared of my extent is but a glimpse of it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परन्तप ।<br>
एष तूद्देशतः प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरो मया ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-41" data-reference="10.41">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-41">10.41</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever being is glorious, prosperous, or mighty, know that it springs from a fragment of my splendor.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा ।<br>
तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसम्भवम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-10-42" data-reference="10.42">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-10-42">10.42</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But what need have you to know all this in detail, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>? I stand supporting this whole world with a single fragment of myself.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन ।<br>
विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
